name: Fresh Basil Crostini
recipeIngredient: French or rustic Italian baguette - thinly sliced, about 1 inch thick
3 large cloves garlic, peeled and minced
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Combine all ingredients (except bread) in a bowl.
3. Brush the mixture onto both sides of the bread slices.
4. Toast on a baking sheet in the preheated oven for 10-15 minutes, or until nicely toasted and crisp.
5. If preparing in advance, cool and store in an air tight container for up to 1 day.
6. For serving, add toppings of choice and serve at room temperature.
Toppings:
- Tomato and bacon: finely chopped tomato (remove seeds and juice), minced shallots seasoned with salt and pepper; topped with crumbled crisp cooked bacon; garnished with small basil leaves.
- Flavored cream cheese or goat cheese: combine log-style goat cheese or cream cheese, minced sun-dried tomatoes (olive oil packed and drained), ground black pepper, fresh basil, and a little olive oil in a food processor fitted with a metal blade.
- Carmelized leeks and tomato: cook chopped leeks (white part only) in olive oil on low heat until lightly browned; combine with cooled chopped tomato (remove seeds and juice), seasoned with salt, pepper, and minced fresh thyme.
- Feta and olives (or tapenade): spread a thin layer of feta cheese processed with whipping cream to make it smooth and spreadable; top with pitted black imported olives (drained) finely chopped and seasoned with minced fresh thyme; garnish with a parsley leaf. Alternatively, use store-bought tapenade.
- Prosciutto and pesto: thinly slice prosciutto; dollop with drained pesto (pesto placed in sieve to save oil); garnish with a parsley leaf.
- Fresh mozzarella and tomato: marinate fresh mozzarella in olive oil, minced garlic, dried red pepper flakes, and salt for up to two weeks; drain but keep marinade coating the exterior; place on crostini and top with minced fresh basil.
- Blue cheese, fresh pear, and pecans: whirl blue cheese with a little whipping cream or crème fraîche; top with a small slice of fresh pear (dipped in lemon water); garnish with a plain or candied pecan half.
